description_long: This large, stately house features a symmetrical facade with a prominent central entrance, flanked by tall columns and a covered porch. The structure is three stories tall, with a steeply pitched, multi-gabled red roof and several dormer windows adding architectural interest. The exterior walls are primarily brown with white-trimmed windows arranged in neat, horizontal rows, and the ground level is surrounded by lush green landscaping. The rear of the house includes a rounded, protruding section, likely a sunroom or formal room, and the sides are marked by vertical columns and additional windows. The roofline is complex, with multiple intersecting gables and dormers, giving the house a grand, historic appearance. The overall design suggests a blend of Colonial Revival and Craftsman influences, with an emphasis on symmetry, formality, and decorative roof elements.
description: A grand, three-story house with a steep red gabled roof, symmetrical facade, and prominent columned entryway, surrounded by lush landscaping. The exterior features brown walls, white-trimmed windows in neat rows, and multiple dormers and gables.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with a columned porch, symmetrical window arrangement, and central gabled roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the house, vertical columns, and rows of windows along the side wall.
description_view_2: Rear view featuring a rounded protruding section, symmetrical window rows, and the continuation of the red gabled roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing additional windows, columns, and a small balcony above a secondary entrance.
attribute_entryway_design: Central entry with wide steps, covered porch supported by tall columns, and decorative overhang
